claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my daughter Alex. She's 6 years old. I'm interested in finding out if I can claim Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know about Alex, who is 6 years old.

Now I need to ask you a few more questions to determine your eligibility:

**Do you currently live in the UK with Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do live in the UK with Alex. She lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. Since Alex is under 16 and lives with you in the UK, those are positive factors for eligibility.

Now I need to ask:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (For example, Alex's other parent or another family member)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me looking after her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that. 

Just to make sure I have all the information I need:

**Are you responsible for Alex's care - meaning you're the one paying for things like her food, clothes, and other everyday needs?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm the one who pays for all of Alex's things - her food, clothes, school stuff, everything she needs really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for providing all that information. Let me just confirm one final point:

**Do you have the right to reside in the UK?** (For example, are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, pre-settled status, or another immigration status that allows you to live here?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a UK citizen. I have the right to live here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 6 years old (under 16) ✓
- You live in the UK with Alex ✓
- You are responsible for her care and pay for her needs ✓
- You are a UK citizen with the right to reside in the UK ✓
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her ✓

You meet all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. 

**One important note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - you can still receive the benefit, but you might need to pay some or all of it back through your tax return.

If you'd like to proceed with claiming Child Benefit for Alex, you'll need to make a claim. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old, which is under 16, so she meets the age requirement. You live in the UK with Alex and are responsible for her care, paying for her food, clothes, and other everyday needs. You are a UK citizen with the right to reside in the UK. Nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. You meet all the standard eligibility criteria: you're responsible for a child under 16, you live with her in the UK, and only one person (you) is claiming for her. Therefore, you qualify for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK with their 6-year-old daughter Alex. They are responsible for Alex's care and pay for all her needs. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.
